title: Uzbekistan at the 2026 World Cup · lineup & formation | MyLineups
original_text:
Drawn into Group K, Uzbekistan head into the 2026 World Cup in a 3-4-2-1, coached by Fabio Cannavaro.

It is a squad drawn from 16 clubs worldwide, averaging 27.1 years, and worth roughly 66.1 million combined. Plot the XI below, then open it in the free builder to shape your own Uzbekistan lineup for the tournament.

Uzbekistan line up for the FIFA World Cup 2026 in Group K in a 3-4-2-1 , with a back 3 and Yusupov in goal.

In goal: Yusupov, Ergashev, and Nematov.

In defence: Ashurmatov, Khusanov, Nasrulloev, Alidzhanov, Ulasmaliev, and Urozov.

In midfield: Abdullaev, Karimov, Mozgovoy, Shomurodov, Shukurov, Urunov, Esanov, Eshmuradov, Ganiev, Iskandarov, Khamdamov, Khamrobekov, and Sayfiev.

In attack: Fayzullayev, Amonov, Jiyanov, and Sergeev.

The 26-man squad pulls players from 16 clubs across world football. The starting XI averages 27.1 years and is worth roughly 66.1 million combined.

Fabio Cannavaro (ITA) is the coach guiding this side.

Uzbekistan stays tied to the World Cup hub and opens directly in the builder if you want to reshape the tournament XI — free on the web or in the mobile app.

Open Uzbekistan in the free MyLineups builder, reshape the 3-4-2-1, pick your captain and export a clean image.
